purpose: Structural DNA of Aristotle's compiled_advance + strategically-valuable disproof artifacts
method: Read ARISTOTLE_SUMMARY.md + Main.lean for each success; classify proof_pattern, key_tactic, sketch-provided data, novelty

successes.problem: Feit-Thompson p=3 prime conjecture for q≡ 71 (mod 72), q ≤ 1000
successes.proof_pattern: Enumerated residue-class subfamily + Fermat-little-theorem reduction with per-prime small-divisor witnesses
successes.key_tactic: interval_cases q; native_decide; not_dvd_via_fermat_factor with explicit (q, p) pairs
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Residue class q ≡ 71 mod 72, bound q≤1000, prior bundle context (slots 612/613 Wieferich)

successes.problem: Brocard's conjecture: ≥4 primes in (p_n², p_{n+1}²) for n ∈ [2,500]
successes.proof_pattern: Computable nthPrime bridge + native_decide on Finset.Icc + Nat.nth_count bridging lemma
successes.key_tactic: native_decide on parametric Finset.Icc 0 N; Nat.nth_count to bridge noncomputable Nat.nth Nat.Prime
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Numeric bound N, target cardinality ≥4, suggestion to verify computationally
successes.key_lemma_signature: nthPrimeComp n = Nat.nth Nat.Prime n for n ≤ N
successes.load_bearing_data: definition of computable nthPrimeComp using Nat.find of next prime; Nat.count Nat.Prime as bridge

successes.problem: FT p=3 q≡71 mod 72 extended to q≤1500 (iter3)
successes.proof_pattern: Bound → Finset.range cast + single native_decide cascade (no per-q case split)
successes.key_tactic: native_decide on Finset.range 1501 with full ∀-reduction via suffices/Finset.mem_range
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Numeric bound q ≤ 1500, residue class, slot720 prior context
successes.load_bearing_data: the bound; native_decide handled actual modular exponentiation

successes.problem: Erdős 647 Sophie Germain residue subclass (proved by case analysis on Cunningham chain split)
successes.proof_pattern: Multiplicative-function lower bound on σ₀ + explicit witness construction (m = n-3 or m = n-4) per disjunctive case
successes.key_tactic: Case split on hsplit; manual sigma₀ lower bounds via Nat.divisors composite ≥3 + multiplicativity (no native_decide on main statement)
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Bounded floor n ≥ 3000, Cunningham residual disjunction ((2q-1)/3 composite OR (q-1)/2 composite), slot723 defensive context
successes.key_lemma_signature: σ₀(3c) ≥ 6 for composite c ≥ 999;  σ₀(4d) ≥ 7 for composite d ≥ 749
successes.load_bearing_data: the case-split hypothesis (the Cunningham disjunction); Aristotle synthesized the multiplicativity lemmas

successes.proof_pattern: Witness-table chunked into 10×50; Bool checker + Nat.nth_count bridge + cardinality lemma
successes.key_tactic: native_decide on each of 10 chunks; checkEntryB function; nth_prime_of_count using Nat.nth_count
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Numeric range [501,1000], 4-witness target cardinality, slot722 context
successes.key_lemma_signature: checkEntryB n entry = true → 4 ≤ ((Finset.Ioo (n.nth Prime^2) ...).filter Prime).card
successes.load_bearing_data: approach reused slot722's bridge pattern; sketch provided the bound, Aristotle precomputed the witness table externally and embedded as List

successes.problem: Existence of a nonabelian Leinster group (D₃ × C₅)
successes.proof_pattern: Coprime-product decomposition of normal subgroups → multiplicativity of σ → finite group enumeration on each factor
successes.key_tactic: Mixed: structural theorem normal_subgroup_of_coprime_prod + native_decide / finite enumeration of normal subgroups of D₃ and C₅
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Existence formulation, candidate witness (slot575 D₃ × C₅ reframe)
successes.key_lemma_signature: Nat.Coprime |G| |H| → every Subgroup (G×H) factors; σ(G×H) = σ G · σ H
successes.load_bearing_data: the explicit witness D₃ × C₅ (vs sketches that left existence open); coprimality of orders 6 and 5

successes.problem: Erdős 203 covering: original conjecture index1_covering_insufficient_M8_B500 disproven
successes.proof_pattern: Greedy set cover on partition structure of 25 multi-cell index-1 primes + CRT realization → explicit 43-digit m + native_decide
successes.key_tactic: native_decide on ∃ _ : ℕ, ∀ k l : Fin 8, ∃ p ∈ Finset.range 501, ...
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Index-1 prime set, bound p ≤ 500, finite grid 8×8, candidate covering structure
successes.key_lemma_signature: ∀ (k,l) ∈ Fin 8 × Fin 8, ∃ prime p ≤ 500: p | 2^k · 3^l · m + 1
successes.load_bearing_data: explicit counterexample_m = 1579554969991861182625270235031094424159694 (computed externally, embedded as def); structure of 22 covering primes

successes.problem: OEIS A67720: φ(k²+1) = k·φ(k+1), bounded search up to 3200
successes.proof_pattern: Bounded universal statement → native_decide on Finset.range, plus structural lemmas (gcd, prime factor congruence mod 4)
successes.load_bearing_data: the bound; the equation was already definable in Mathlib

successes.problem: Erdős 137 k=3: product of 3 consecutive integers not powerful, bounded verification
successes.proof_pattern: Bounded native_decide + structural decomposition by parity/divisibility-by-3 case-split
successes.key_tactic: native_decide on bounded range + manual divisibility lemmas
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: k=3 specialization, Powerful definition, bound n < 10000
successes.load_bearing_data: the bound; the parity case-split structure

successes.problem: Casas-Alvero conjecture in positive characteristic p (counterexample)
successes.proof_pattern: Explicit polynomial witness X^(p+1) - X^p over ZMod p + structural Lean proofs of monic / degree / Hasse derivative properties
successes.key_tactic: Polynomial.hasseDeriv manipulation; explicit construction; standard polynomial lemmas in Mathlib
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Casas-Alvero property definition, characteristic p hint, polynomial structure
successes.load_bearing_data: the explicit polynomial witness (Daniel Lazard's counterexample is classical)

successes.problem: FT p=3 q≡71 mod 72 synthesis contradiction (early arc, slot720 predecessor)
successes.proof_pattern: Derived modular formula k mod A + numerical check that derived value differs from actual at q=71 → contradicts 3^q ≡ 1 mod A
successes.load_bearing_data: the algebraic identity and explicit q=71 numeric check

successes.problem: Lehmer's Totient Problem: counterexample must be odd + squarefree (structural)
successes.db_status: disproven (negative structural result on shape of counterexample)
successes.proof_pattern: Manual structural number-theoretic proof (no native_decide). Squarefreeness from p² | n ⇒ p | φ(n); odd from φ even on evens
successes.load_bearing_data: the definition; classical results in Mathlib

successes.problem: Erdős 850: same-prime-factors triple constraint  — structural partial results
successes.db_status: disproven (negative bounds: no x<10, diff≥30, divisible by 6, etc.)
successes.proof_pattern: Bounded native_decide search up to small N + structural deductions (rad-lcm divisibility, parity, smoothness)
successes.key_tactic: native_decide on bounded x range + radical/rad-lcm structural lemmas
successes.load_bearing_data: the bound for search; definition of rad/rad_lcm

successes.problem: Erdős 672 k=3 l=2: product of 3 terms in AP is a perfect square (counterexample (1,24))
successes.proof_pattern: Single explicit AP witness (n=1, d=24) → product = 1·25·49 = 1225 = 35² → native_decide
successes.load_bearing_data: the (1,24) witness was needed; Aristotle (or context) supplied it

successes.proof_pattern: Constructive 8-vertex graph G_ex + Cycle4 verification + triangle T1_ex = {0,2,6} witnessing the violation
successes.key_tactic: Explicit Fintype graph + decide on adjacencies + manual triangle membership
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Diagonal exclusion hypothesis statement + Cycle4 definition

successes.problem: Tuza external triangles sharing common apex (FALSE)
successes.proof_pattern: Constructive 10-vertex graph with explicit maxPacking M_cex = {A,B,C,D} + two disjoint external triangles T1, T2
successes.key_tactic: Explicit Finset construction + decide on packing-number = 4
successes.data_provided_in_sketch: Hypothesis about external triangles + Cycle4 definition + slot262 packing definitions

snipe_signatures.description: ∀ n ≤ N, P(n) where P is decidable in Mathlib → reduce ∀ ℕ to Finset.range (N+1) and native_decide
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: an explicit numeric bound N for the universal quantifier; P(n) must be expressible using existing Mathlib decidable predicates (Nat.Prime, totient, divisors, modular conditions)
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 4+ slots, all clean compiles, axiom count typically just standard
snipe_signatures.limit: fails when modular exponent or search blow up native_decide budget

snipe_signatures.description: Universal claim over indices 0..N involving noncomputable Nat.nth + verification: precompute witness table externally, chunk it (10×50 typical), and bridge to Nat.nth via Nat.nth_count
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: Numeric range, target cardinality / property of witness, and ideally a parametrized statement using Nat.nth Nat.Prime
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 2 slots in different N ranges using the same scaffold; explicitly reused encoding in slot738
snipe_signatures.limit: scales linearly in N; chunking required to stay under recursion depth

snipe_signatures.description: FT-style: for q in arithmetic progression q≡r mod M, enumerate q via interval_cases and supply (q,p) Fermat-divisor pairs; use 3^(q mod (p-1)) ≢ 1 mod p as a closure
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: Residue class (q≡r mod M), bound q≤N, prior context indicating which small primes p_i divide A(q_i)
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 2 slots; slot720 is a clean generalization of the slot633 single-instance attack
snipe_signatures.limit: needs a small prime divisor of A(q) below the threshold where Fermat reduction is feasible; breaks when A(q) is prime (e.g. predicted at q=1583)

snipe_signatures.description: For a finite-grid coverage / set-cover problem, run greedy externally, realize via CRT, embed the resulting big integer as a def, and verify the cover by native_decide on the finite grid
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: Finite grid bounds, prime set, divisibility predicate; the explicit witness integer (or hints that one can be computed externally)
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 1 slot but mechanism is reusable for any 2D covering / set-cover problem
snipe_signatures.limit: produces only finite-grid witness; lifting to infinite case requires a separate periodic-lattice argument (open in E203)

snipe_signatures.description: Existence statement ∃ m < n: f(m) > bound, prove by disjunctive case split with witness m ∈ {n-1, n-2, n-3, n-4} per case, plus manual multiplicativity lower bounds on σ₀
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: The bound, the disjunctive hypothesis (e.g. Cunningham split), the divisor-function lower-bound target
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 2 slots in the same E647 family; pattern explicitly compounded across iterations
snipe_signatures.limit: requires the disjunction to actually cover the residue case; misses when none of the offset witnesses work (the truly hard Cunningham residue)

snipe_signatures.description: Combinatorial disproof: construct an explicit small graph (8 to 12 vertices), define adjacency by decide, verify all required structural conditions, exhibit the witness triangle/packing
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: Precise hypothesis to refute; the conjectured structural property; Cycle4/packing definitions in context
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 4 slots in the Tuza arc; each refuted a candidate bridging lemma
snipe_signatures.limit: specialty in graph combinatorics; counterexample search is hard for larger structures

snipe_signatures.description: Algebraic existence: explicit polynomial / group element / coset witness, then verify standard property using Mathlib's library (Polynomial.hasseDeriv, Subgroup.Normal, ZMod, DihedralGroup)
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: The explicit witness (P = X^(p+1) - X^p; D₃×C₅; (n,d)=(1,24)); the property definition
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 3 slots across algebra and nt; reliable when witness is classical or supplied
snipe_signatures.limit: novelty is in the witness, not the proof; Aristotle won't discover novel algebraic witnesses, but verifies supplied ones

snipe_signatures.description: For an open existence conjecture, produce necessary conditions on any counterexample (must be odd, squarefree, divisible by 6, diff ≥ 30) without resolving existence
snipe_signatures.sketch_data_required: The counterexample definition; permission to bound the search
snipe_signatures.reproducibility_evidence: 3 slots across Lehmer, Erdős 850, Erdős 137; produces 'compiled-no-advance' or 'disproven' tags rather than full resolution
snipe_signatures.limit: doesn't actually close the gap, only narrows it; user has flagged these as not 'gap-resolutions'

top3_reproducibility: S1_bounded_native_decide_universal (4 slots; pattern: bound + decidable predicate)
S6_explicit_finite_graph_counterexample (4 slots; pattern: small Fintype graph + decide)
S5_case_split_with_explicit_small_offset_witnesses (2 slots same arc; pattern: σ₀ lower bounds + offset witness)
